steps.title: Identify the hypothesis's prediction
steps.content: The second team predicts that in low-visibility conditions, wider-headed fish will be less likely than narrower-headed fish to detect obstructions.

steps.title: Select evidence matching the prediction
steps.content:
Choice B makes the required comparison: the wide-headed bristlemouth bumped into more obstructions than the narrow-headed smooth-hound in medium-vortex, low-visibility conditions.

Choices A, C, and D either lack this head-type comparison or show equal detection ability between head types.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
